guild_order_material_policy.notes: V1 supports standard-order submit / skip / refresh only. When a self-select custom order is visible, the runtime preserves evidence and records a truthful skip instead of widening into candidate selection.

decision_rules.submit: Visible standard requirements are allowed and sufficiently stocked from bounded evidence only.
decision_rules.skip: Visible order is already completed, the state is still ambiguous, custom-order handling is disabled, refresh is disallowed, or no truthful bounded action remains after policy evaluation.
decision_rules.refresh: Visible standard-order evidence is unsuitable and one bounded refresh remains allowed by policy.

guild_order_decision_contract.notes: Unknown order state must fail or skip honestly instead of inventing a hidden action path. V1 keeps custom-order handling outside scope and records that surface as a truthful skip.

guild_order_visibility_contract.notes: V1 uses bounded runtime OCR evidence and signature-based verification instead of template-blocked quantity contracts, but still preserves truthful supporting scene metadata for future evidence work.

do_not_click_notes: Do not use the top-right line-switch arrow near the minimap as the merchant entry.
Do not reopen Carnival for every round after one Meow Group commission is already accepted.
Do not widen the route to any non-Meow merchant group.
merchant_commission_meow_route_contract.notes: Validated route stays inside the fixed Poring -> Carnival entry and the preferred left task-list re-entry loop.

merchant_commission_meow_loop_contract.notes: The validated Meow Group loop is a semi-fixed 10-round material hand-in chain with immediate-buy support.

merchant_commission_meow_submission_poli.notes: The first delivery slice may buy missing materials from the validated submit panel, but it must not widen into crafting, gathering, or other merchant-group routes.

merchant_commission_meow_decision_contra.notes: The round resolver only chooses among the validated submit, immediate-buy-then-submit, or stop-for-operator outcomes.

implementation_requirements.summary: Odin preset entry requires an idle-state calibration profile before runtime binding is safe.
